summary: Arqel dogfood loop — Round 7: re-detect against main (47 bugs resolved/triaged rounds 0-4). Deepest probes into the least-covered surfaces (audit attribute filtering, realtime broadcast dispatch, mcp tools, cli generators, export xlsx/formatting, i18n/locale, theme, command palette, a11y, edge-case interactions between traits). Seen-filtered (52 sigs), adversarial verify. Stop after 2 consecutive clean rounds.
logs: Round 7 detection: 9 from clusters = 9 after dedup/seen-filter
Round 7 verify: 8 confirmed NEW framework bugs, 0 app-misuse, 1 not-a-bug

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: arqel:widget generator scaffolds classes that extend final widget base classes — every generated widget is a fatal error on autoload
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/widgets/stubs/widgets/stat.stub:9 (and chart.stub:9, table.stub:9, custom.stub:9) — they emit `final class {{class}} extends {Stat|Chart|Table|Custom}Widget`, while packages/widgets/src/StatWidget.php:34, ChartWidget.php:27, TableWidget.php:27, CustomWidget.php:29 all declare the base `final`.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Make the three concrete data-bearing bases (StatWidget/ChartWidget/TableWidget) non-final so generated subclasses load, and change custom.stub to compose `CustomWidget::make($name, $component)` (a registrar/provider stub) instead of subclassing the final escape-hatch class. Then add a test that autoloads/instantiates the generated class for each --type to catch regressions.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced concretely. (1) All four concrete base classes are declared `final` (StatWidget.php:34, ChartWidget.php:27, TableWidget.php:27, CustomWidget.php:29). (2) All four stubs render `final class {{class}} extends {Base}Widget`. (3) MakeWidgetCommand.php:71-72 renders exactly these stubs per `--type`; `arqel:widget` is the documented generation path (docblock lines 17-21). (4) Direct PHP repro: declaring a class extending final StatWidget yields `Fatal error: Class App\Widgets\DemoBugWidget cannot extend final class Arqel\Widgets\StatWidget`. So every generated widget fatals the moment its class is declared/autoloaded — any Dashboard registering it (`DemoBugWidget::make()` / `new`) or discovery referencing it dies. (5) The existing test MakeWidgetCommandTest.php only does string assertions (`toContain('extends StatWidget')`, lines 49/68/98) and never instantiates/autoloads the class, so it codifies the bug instead of catching it. (6) The custom.stub case is doubly wrong: CustomWidget's own docblock states it is an escape-hatch usable 'without scaffolding a dedicated PHP subclass', yet the stub subclasses it. Confirmed committed on the active branch (no uncommitted widgets changes) and apps/showcase consumes arqel-dev/widgets ^0.12.0 via path symlink. This is intended-final design colliding with subclass-emitting stubs — a framework bug, not app misuse, and not a deferred/intended behavior. Severity blocker: 100% of the generator's output across all 4 types is dead-on-arrival.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: MakeWidgetCommand renders stubs in packages/widgets/stubs/widgets/{stat,chart,table,custom}.stub, each declaring `final class {{class}} extends {Stat|Chart|Table|Custom}Widget`. But all four concrete base classes are declared `final` in packages/widgets/src/: StatWidget.php:34 `final class StatWidget`, ChartWidget.php:27, TableWidget.php:27, CustomWidget.php:29. Running `php artisan arqel:widget DemoBugWidget --type=stat` then autoloading the class yields: 'Fatal error: Class App\Widgets\DemoBugWidget cannot extend final class Arqel\Widgets\StatWidget in app/Widgets/DemoBugWidget.php on line 9'. Reproduced for all four --type values (stat/chart/table/custom) in apps/showcase. The generator is the documented way to create widgets, so 100% of its output is dead-on-arrival the moment Laravel discovers the class. CustomWidget's own docblock even states it is an 'escape-hatch... without scaffolding a dedicated PHP subclass', yet the custom stub subclasses it. The existing test packages/widgets/tests/Feature/MakeWidgetCommandTest.php only asserts the source string contains 'extends StatWidget' (line 49/68/98) and never instantiates the class, so it codifies rather than catches the bug. Contrast: arqel:dashboard (Dashboard::make composition), arqel:field (extends abstract Field), arqel:make-user, arqel:introspect all verified working end-to-end. Likely fix: make the stat/chart/table base classes non-final (or have stubs extend the abstract Widget base and compose), and have the custom stub use CustomWidget::make() composition rather than subclassing.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: readonly()/disabled() fields are persisted on write (mass-assignment bypass): pruneUnauthorizedFields only checks canBeEditedBy, which ignores isReadonly()/isDisabled()
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/core/src/Http/Controllers/ResourceController.php:494-508 (pruneUnauthorizedFields); root contract violated at packages/fields/src/Field.php:235-241 (isDehydrated) never consulted on the write path
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In pruneUnauthorizedFields, after the canBeEditedBy loop, add a pass that unset($data[$name]) for any field where method_exists($field,'isDehydrated') && $field->isDehydrated($record) === false (and, per Filament semantics, for isDisabled($record)===true unless explicitly re-dehydrated). This enforces the documented dehydrated(false) "Don't persist" contract server-side.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced at runtime against packages/fields on current main: (new TextField('locked'))->readonly() yields isReadonly()=true but canBeEditedBy(null,null)=true; ->disabled() yields isDisabled()=true, canBeEditedBy=true; ->dehydrated(false) yields isDehydrated()=false, canBeEditedBy=true. ResourceController::pruneUnauthorizedFields (lines 494-508) drops a submitted value ONLY when canBeEditedBy() is false (the #102 fix) plus layout-visibility (#115). HasAuthorization::canBeEditedBy returns true whenever no canSee/canEdit closure is set and never consults isReadonly/isDisabled/isDehydrated. Grep confirms isDehydrated() is consulted NOWHERE in production code (only PHPStan cache artifacts) and not in Resource::runCreate/runUpdate, which do fill($data)->save() (Resource.php:268,288). FieldSchemaSerializer serializes readonly/disabled to the client (lines 66-67), so a developer marking a field display-only reasonably assumes server-side rejection. The decisive evidence is PLANNING/05-api-php.md:285, which documents ->dehydrated(false) as 'Don't persist' — a public, documented persistence-control contract with zero server enforcement. Both persist paths are exploitable: with arqel-dev/form installed the field's rules are still extracted from effectiveFields() so a value with any rule survives $request->validate() into $validated; without form, validated() falls back to $request->except([...]) returning the entire raw input. Either way the value passes pruneUnauthorizedFields and is fill()+save()d. This is a genuine mass-assignment bypass for the documented display-only/computed-field pattern, distinct from the #102 fix (canEdit(false)/canSee(false)). It is a framework bug, not app misuse: the framework ships and documents these modifiers as the way to make a field non-persisting; honoring them is the framework's responsibility. Severity high (not blocker): exploitation requires the developer to (a) use readonly/disabled/dehydrated AND (b) leave the attribute in $fillable; a $fillable allowlist still bounds the blast radius, and the showcase baseline does not currently use these modifiers — so impact is conditional on app config rather than universal. Eligible distinct-bug criteria met: documented contract (dehydrated 'Don't persist') is concretely violated; not a deferred feature.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: ResourceController::pruneUnauthorizedFields (packages/core/src/Http/Controllers/ResourceController.php:459-511) drops a submitted value only when $field->canBeEditedBy($user,$record) is false. HasAuthorization::canBeEditedBy (packages/fields/src/Concerns/HasAuthorization.php:54-65) returns true whenever no canSee/canEdit closure is set — it never consults isReadonly() or isDisabled(). Verified at runtime: (new TextField('locked'))->readonly() gives isReadonly()=true but canBeEditedBy(null,null)=true; (new TextField('d'))->disabled() also gives canBeEditedBy=true. FieldSchemaSerializer.php:66 serializes readonly:true to the client, so a user who marks a field ->readonly() (the documented display-only pattern) reasonably assumes the server rejects writes — but the value flows through validated() into Resource::runCreate/runUpdate's fill($data)->save() (Resource.php:268,288) and is persisted. Distinct from the #102 fix, which only covers canEdit(false)/canSee(false).

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: File upload/delete endpoints perform no per-resource authorization: any authenticated panel user can write/delete files to any resource's configured disk
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In store(): before writing, resolve the owner model and call the same Gate authorize used by ResourceController ('create'/'update' on $instance::getModel()), plus the field-level $fieldInstance->canBeEditedBy($request->user(), $record) oracle; abort(403) on deny. In destroy(): authorize 'update'/'delete' the same way AND constrain $path to the field's configured directory (reject paths escaping getDirectory() / containing '..') before Storage::delete, so an arbitrary disk path cannot be removed.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Confirmed by reading the controller on main. FieldUploadController::store (lines 39-80) and ::destroy (lines 82-99) resolve resource+field then call $upload->store(...) and Storage::disk($disk)->delete($path) with NO Gate/Policy/authorize() call and no field-level canBeEditedBy check. This is a parallel write path into the same resource's configured disk that bypasses the resource-level authorization the canonical ResourceController enforces on every write (authorize('create'/'update'/'delete', ...) at lines 51/60/69/96/107/123) and the field-level write pruning (#102 pruneUnauthorizedFields). Field routes share the panel middleware (FieldServiceProvider::registerFieldRoutes, default ['web']) exactly like resource routes, so any authenticated panel user reaches them regardless of per-resource permissions. destroy() additionally takes a raw 'path' form input and deletes it with no containment check against the field's configured directory, giving an arbitrary-file-delete primitive on the disk; the existing FieldUploadControllerTest passes the user-controlled path straight through and asserts deletion, with zero authorization assertions in the whole test file. This is distinct from the swept Gate-vs-Policy family (there the controller called authorize but with the wrong oracle); here there is simply no authorization call at all. Not deferred/intended: resource-level (RF-AU-01) and field-level (RF-AU-03) authorization are Phase-1 priority-1 requirements; the only deferred upload item is presigned-URL uploads (planning line 2060), unrelated to authorization. The controller docstring claims only authentication, never authorization. Severity high (not blocker) because exploitation requires an authenticated panel session and a FileField/ImageField in use, but it yields privilege escalation on writes and a destructive arbitrary-delete primitive.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: FieldUploadController::store and ::destroy (packages/fields/src/Http/Controllers/FieldUploadController.php:39-99) resolve the resource+field then call $upload->store(...) / Storage::disk(...)->delete($path) with no authorize() / Gate / Policy check for create or update on the owner resource. Routes (packages/fields/routes/arqel-fields.php:25-30) are guarded only by panel middleware (default ['web']). A user with no create/update permission on a resource can still POST a file to its disk/directory, and DELETE with an arbitrary path form field removes any file under the configured disk (no path containment check). Distinct from the swept Gate-vs-Policy family — there is simply no authorization call at all.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: dehydrated(false) field values are still persisted: the write path never consults Field::isDehydrated()
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/core/src/Http/Controllers/ResourceController.php:459 (pruneUnauthorizedFields — never consults isDehydrated); persistence at Resource.php:268/288 (fill($data)->save())
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In ResourceController::pruneUnauthorizedFields(), after the canBeEditedBy prune loop, drop any field where method_exists($field,'isDehydrated') && !$field->isDehydrated($record) — mirroring the spec's prescribed filterDehydrated() step (PLANNING/08-fase-1-mvp.md:3804).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced at runtime. Write path is ResourceController::store/update -> validated() -> pruneUnauthorizedFields() -> Resource::runCreate/runUpdate (fill($data)->save()). grep confirms isDehydrated()/dehydrat appears NOWHERE in core/src, form/src (FieldRulesExtractor), or FieldSchemaSerializer — only in Field.php and FieldTest.php. A Pest reproduction with a duck-typed field returning isDehydrated()=false and canBeEditedBy()=true passed pruning untouched and persisted the forged client value 'forged-client-value' to the DB (assertion $record->secret === 'forged-client-value' passed). The serializer also ignores isDehydrated(), so the field still renders as an editable input. This is NOT a deferred feature: PLANNING/08-fase-1-mvp.md:1548 lists 'dehydrated(false) previne persistência' as a Phase-1 MVP acceptance criterion, line 3804-3805 prescribes an explicit filterDehydrated() step in the create flow that the controller omits, and line 3958 marks testing it as critical; PLANNING/05-api-php.md:285 documents '->dehydrated(false) // Dont persist'. So a documented Phase-1 public contract is violated — framework bug, not app misuse. Severity downgraded from suspected high to medium: it requires an uncommon dehydrated(false) declaration, the failure mode is forged/extra data written (not auth bypass — auth-restricted fields are still pruned by canBeEditedBy), and model $guarded/$fillable is a user-side backstop. A computed/display-only field's client value reaching the DB is a correctness violation but not a security blocker.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: Field::isDehydrated() exists and is documented as controlling whether a field's value is saved (packages/fields/src/Field.php:235-242; class doc line 29 'dehydration'). grep across core/src, fields/src, form/src shows isDehydrated() is called NOWHERE on the write path — not in ResourceController (validated/pruneUnauthorizedFields), not in Resource::runCreate/runUpdate, not in FieldRulesExtractor. Verified at runtime: (new TextField('computed'))->dehydrated(false) gives isDehydrated()=false but canBeEditedBy(null,null)=true, so prune keeps it; it then reaches fill($data)->save(). A field declared dehydrated(false) (computed/display-only, Filament semantics: excluded from saved data) has its forged client value written to the DB.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: BelongsTo search endpoint performs no per-resource authorization: any authenticated panel user can enumerate/exfiltrate any related model's records
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Before querying the related model, run the framework's existing authorization gate against the related Resource's model — mirror ResourceController::authorize() by calling Gate (viewAny on $relatedModelClass; or view on each candidate) and abort(403) when Gate::getPolicyFor($relatedModelClass) exists and denies. Reuse/extract the ResourceController::authorize() helper (Gate::has || getPolicyFor guard + Gate::denies => abort 403) so the search endpoint honors related-model Policies exactly like CRUD does.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Confirmed by reading source on current main (HEAD c48eace). FieldSearchController::__invoke (packages/fields/src/Http/Controllers/FieldSearchController.php:40-109) resolves the owner Resource + field, then queries the RELATED model directly (relatedModelClass::query()->...->get(), lines 58-84) and returns {value,label} pairs including recordTitle-derived labels (lines 96-105). It performs NO Gate/authorize/Policy check for either the owner or the related Resource — the only guards are 400 (not a BelongsToField), 403 (search explicitly disabled), and throttle:30,1 (routes/arqel-fields.php:23). The route group is mounted (FieldServiceProvider::registerFieldRoutes, lines 79-99) under the panel's middleware, which defaults to ['web'] (Panel.php:37) — authentication only, no can:/authorize. This is an absence-of-authorization, categorically different from the previously-swept Gate::has-vs-getPolicyFor family (which concerned the shape of an existing check). The framework's own convention, set by ResourceController, is to enforce the model Policy (viewAny/view) when one exists and silently allow otherwise (authorize() at lines 354-366; called on every CRUD method at 51/60/69/86/96/106/123/157). The search endpoint bypasses that contract: a developer who writes a viewAny/view Policy on the related model (the sanctioned auth mechanism, honored by CRUD) has it fully ignored here, so any authenticated panel user can GET {panel}/{resource}/fields/{field}/search?q= and exfiltrate labels (names/emails) of records they cannot view. The existing test FieldSearchControllerTest.php only asserts the search-disabled 403 — no authorization test exists, confirming it was never wired. Caveats lowering severity to medium (not a clean baseline repro): the showcase app ships NO Policies and uses static Field::select('author_id')->options(...) rather than a searchable BelongsToField (PostResource.php:83), so the endpoint is neither reachable nor policy-bypassing as-shipped; reproduction requires the normal usage path of (a) a searchable BelongsToField with a related Resource, (b) a Policy on the related model, and (c) an authenticated user. The leak is read-only labels, authenticated, throttled, and bounded (max 20 per query, though empty/iterative q enumerates broadly). It is a genuine framework-level missing-authorization defect relative to the framework's own authorization contract, and the fix primitive (ResourceController::authorize()) already exists and simply isn't invoked here. Classified framework-bug because the vulnerable code, route mounting, and default middleware all live in packages/fields + packages/core, not in app code.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: FieldSearchController::__invoke (packages/fields/src/Http/Controllers/FieldSearchController.php:40-109) resolves the owner resource and field, then queries the *related* model directly (relatedModelClass::query()->...->get()) and returns {value,label} pairs — with ZERO authorization. It never calls Gate / authorize() / a Policy for either the owner Resource (viewAny/update) or the related Resource. The route (packages/fields/routes/arqel-fields.php:21-23) is mounted only under the panel middleware, which defaults to ['web'] (Panel.php:37) — no can:/authorize gate. By contrast ResourceController authorizes every CRUD method. So any logged-in user can hit GET {panel}/{resource}/fields/{field}/search?q= to dump labels (e.g. user names/emails via recordTitle) of a related resource they have no view permission on. This is absence-of-authorization, categorically different from the swept Gate::has-vs-getPolicyFor family.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.signature: NavigationCommandProvider::provide ignores $user; no viewAny gate before emitting nav Command per ResourceRegistry::all()
confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Command palette (Cmd+K) navigation entries are NOT gated by Resource viewAny Policy — leaks/links Resources the user cannot view
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/core/src/CommandPalette/Providers/NavigationCommandProvider.php:47-60 (provide() ignores $user; emits one nav Command per ResourceRegistry::all() with no viewAny gate). Secondary: packages/core/src/CommandPalette/CommandRegistry.php:121-155 (resolveFor/isVisibleTo apply only requiresAuth/hideForAuthenticated, never a Policy).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In NavigationCommandProvider::provide(), skip a resource when its model is viewAny-denied for $user, mirroring HandleArqelInertiaRequests::resourceViewAnyDenied(): try $modelClass = $resourceClass::getModel(); if (Gate::has('viewAny') || Gate::getPolicyFor($modelClass)) { if (Gate::forUser($user)->denies('viewAny', $modelClass)) continue; }. (Inject Gate; same guard used by the Round-6 sidebar fix so behavior is symmetric and scaffold apps with no policy stay unaffected.)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced in code, not refuted. NavigationCommandProvider is registered unconditionally in ArqelServiceProvider::boot() (lines 220-221); the GET /admin/commands endpoint (routes/admin.php, behind web+auth) passes the authenticated user into CommandRegistry::resolveFor(); resolveFor only applies isVisibleTo() which checks the requiresAuth/hideForAuthenticated presence flags and never a Policy; NavigationCommandProvider::provide() accepts ?Authenticatable $user but never reads it, emitting a 'Go to {label}' command with url /admin/{slug} for EVERY class in ResourceRegistry::all(). This is the SAME authz-disclosure class fixed for the sidebar in commit 8f3eaf2 ('fix(core): gate sidebar navigation by the viewAny Policy'), whose HandleArqelInertiaRequests::resourceViewAnyDenied() (lines 264-279) calls Gate::forUser($user)->denies('viewAny', $modelClass). The two navigation surfaces are now inconsistent: a user denied viewAny sees the resource hidden in the sidebar but still sees its existence + a direct shortcut in the command palette. The provider docblock calls Policy-gating 'deferred to follow-up work', but the shipped sidebar fix post-dates and contradicts that stance, so this is an unswept parallel surface, not an intended/documented deferral (unlike EXPORT-007/008). Severity is medium rather than high: this is metadata DISCLOSURE / resource enumeration, not an access bypass — ResourceController::index still enforces $this->authorize('viewAny', ...) (line 51), so following the leaked link yields 403. No viewAny/Policy assertions exist anywhere in the command-palette tests (NavigationCommandProviderTest, AuthAwareFilterTest only cover requiresAuth/hideForAuthenticated), confirming the gap is untested. Note: the showcase baseline app defines no policies/viewAny gates, so it exhibits no concrete leak today (the Gate::has/getPolicyFor guard short-circuits to 'show all'); the bug bites any consuming app that defines a viewAny Policy — i.e. it is a real framework-level regression of the Round-6 invariant.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: NavigationCommandProvider::provide() (packages/core/src/CommandPalette/Providers/NavigationCommandProvider.php) iterates ResourceRegistry::all() and emits a 'Go to {label}' command (url '/admin/{slug}') for EVERY registered Resource. Its own docblock admits 'we do not yet gate navigation by Policy' and the injected ?Authenticatable $user is accepted but never consulted. CommandRegistry::resolveFor() (packages/core/src/CommandPalette/CommandRegistry.php:121-137) only applies isVisibleTo(), which checks the per-command requiresAuth/hideForAuthenticated presence flags — never a viewAny Policy. By contrast the sidebar nav in HandleArqelInertiaRequests::resourceViewAnyDenied() (packages/core/src/Http/Middleware/HandleArqelInertiaRequests.php:264-279) DOES call Gate::forUser($user)->denies('viewAny', $modelClass) and skips denied resources. So a user who cannot viewAny a Resource sees it hidden in the sidebar (Round 6 fix) but still sees its existence + a direct navigation shortcut in the command palette. This is the same authz-disclosure class as the Round-6 sidebar fix, on a parallel navigation surface that was left unswept — not the previously-swept Gate::has-vs-Policy family.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Presence channel_pattern config is half-wired: resolver honors it but the broadcast auth route hardcodes the default, so any customization breaks presence authorization
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In routes/channels.php, derive the presence channel registration pattern from config (Broadcast::channel(str_replace(['{resource}','{recordId}'],['{resource}','{recordId}'], config('arqel-realtime.presence.channel_pattern', 'arqel.presence.{resource}.{recordId}')), $callback)) so the authorization route matches whatever PresenceChannelResolver emits — i.e. read the same config key both places. Note: Laravel placeholder names ({resource}/{recordId}) are positional-by-name, so a custom pattern must keep those two placeholder tokens; document that constraint.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Confirmed against packages/realtime/src on current main. (1) grep shows 'arqel-realtime.presence.channel_pattern' is read in exactly ONE code path: PresenceChannelResolver::forResource() (src/Presence/PresenceChannelResolver.php:39-51), which builds the channel name a client subscribes to. (2) routes/channels.php:59 registers the presence authorization callback under the hardcoded literal 'arqel.presence.{resource}.{recordId}' and never reads config. This file is loaded statically via Spatie package-tools ->hasRoute('channels') in RealtimeServiceProvider; no config-driven re-registration exists. (3) The config comment (config/arqel-realtime.php:52-56) states channel_pattern 'Configures the presence channel exposed by routes/channels.php', and SKILL.md:200 explicitly says the resolver output is 'idêntico ao registrado em routes/channels.php' — both promise parity that does not exist. (4) Under a custom pattern (a documented, env-adjacent knob with its own resolver + unit tests, e.g. PresenceChannelResolverTest.php:19), the resolver emits e.g. 'tenant.orders:7' while Laravel only has 'arqel.presence.{resource}.{recordId}' registered; Laravel matches subscription auth requests against registered channel patterns, so a non-matching name is denied -> presence subscription silently fails. The default pattern path works, so this is customization-gated, not always-broken. This is a genuine half-wired feature the package author added beyond the RT-004 ticket (the ticket itself only specs the literal), not a deferred stub: the config, env override path, resolver, and tests all exist; only the authorization route wiring is missing. Fails closed (denies, not over-authorizes), so no security/data-integrity escalation. Severity medium: silent breakage of a configurable advanced (Phase 3) feature for any app that exercises the documented customization.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: realtime/config/arqel-realtime.php (lines 52-62) documents 'presence.channel_pattern' as 'Configures the presence channel exposed by routes/channels.php' and PresenceChannelResolver::forResource() (realtime/src/Presence/PresenceChannelResolver.php:40) reads config('arqel-realtime.presence.channel_pattern') to BUILD the channel name a client subscribes to. But routes/channels.php:59 registers the authorization callback under a LITERAL string Broadcast::channel('arqel.presence.{resource}.{recordId}', ...) and never reads the config. grep confirms channel_pattern is read in exactly one place (the resolver) and nowhere in routes/. Effect: if an app overrides presence.channel_pattern (a documented, env-overridable knob), the resolver emits e.g. 'myapp.presence.posts.5' while Broadcast still only authorizes 'arqel.presence.{resource}.{recordId}'. The presence subscription auth request then hits an unregistered channel and is denied — presence silently stops working. The config comment explicitly promises it configures the channels.php channel, which it does not.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.signature: SessionResolver::switchTo stores getKey() but resolve()->findByIdentifier() queries by identifierColumn
confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: SessionResolver loses the active tenant after switch when identifier_column is not the primary key
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In SessionResolver::switchTo(), store the identifier column value, not the PK: app('session')->put($this->sessionKey, $this->identifierFor($tenant)); (and update SessionResolverSwitchTest.php:79 expectation from int 7 to string '7').
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Concretely reproduced with a DB-backed Testbench test (in-memory SQLite, real AbstractTenantResolver::findByIdentifier()): a SessionResolver constructed with identifierColumn='slug' stores $tenant->getKey() (PK=1) in the session on switchTo(), then resolve() reads it back and runs where('slug', '1')->first() -> no row -> null. The active tenant is silently lost on the next request, which is exactly the #81 symptom the override was meant to fix, for any non-PK identifier column. Applying the proposed fix (identifierFor($tenant)) makes the repro pass and the primary regression test ('persists the switched tenant...') still passes; only the strict-typed sibling assertion SessionResolverSwitchTest.php:79 needs int->string adjustment (cosmetic, resolve() coerces to string anyway). The existing regression test masks the bug because its anonymous subclass overrides findByIdentifier() to match on getKey() and always passes identifierColumn='id', so it never exercises the real column mismatch. This is a genuine self-contradicting contract inside a public shipped framework resolver (switchTo writes by getKey, resolve reads by identifierColumn) and TenantServiceProvider::buildConfiguredResolver() does forward config('arqel.tenancy.identifier_column') into the constructor, so a SessionResolver-configured app with identifier_column!=id would trigger it. Severity is low rather than medium because the evidence's claim that the showcase wires SessionResolver is INACCURATE: apps/showcase/config/arqel.php sets resolver=AuthUserResolver, which resolves via the user's relation (not findByIdentifier) and is immune; grep shows SessionResolver is referenced only by its own unit tests, so no current baseline app path triggers it. The bug is therefore latent/dormant in the framework, not active in the post-47 showcase. Not refutable — it is a real defect, just lower real-world impact than stated.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: SessionResolver::switchTo() (packages/tenant/src/Resolvers/SessionResolver.php:50-53) persists $tenant->getKey() (the PRIMARY KEY) into the session key. But SessionResolver::resolve() (lines 26-39) reads that session value and calls AbstractTenantResolver::findByIdentifier() (packages/tenant/src/Resolvers/AbstractTenantResolver.php:66-78), which queries where($this->identifierColumn, $value). The identifierColumn is wired from config('arqel.tenancy.identifier_column') by TenantServiceProvider::buildConfiguredResolver() (line 115) — and the showcase config sets it to 'slug' (apps/showcase/config/arqel.php:34). With identifier_column='slug', switchTo stores the numeric PK but resolve runs where('slug', <numeric-pk>) → no row → tenant silently lost on the next request, reproducing the #81 symptom the override was meant to fix, for any non-PK identifier column. The existing regression test SessionResolverSwitchTest.php uses a custom findByIdentifier that matches on getKey(), so it never exercises the real identifierColumn mismatch. switchTo should store $tenant->getAttribute($identifierColumn) (i.e. identifierFor($tenant)), not getKey().

notBugs.title: AiManager::embed() enforces cost limits but never records cost or dispatches AiCompletionGenerated, so embedding spend is invisible to daily/per-user limits
notBugs.reasoning:
Code behavior reproduces exactly: AiManager::embed() (packages/ai/src/AiManager.php:104-112) calls $this->costTracker?->assertWithinLimit($userId) then returns $provider->embed($text), never calling record() nor dispatching AiCompletionGenerated, unlike complete()/chat(). However this is intended/structural design, not a defect:

1) Structural impossibility: AiProvider::embed() contract (Contracts/AiProvider.php) returns array<int,float> — a raw vector with NO token/cost/model data. CostTracker::record() REQUIRES an AiCompletionResult (model, inputTokens, outputTokens, estimatedCost). There is no AiCompletionResult produced by embed, so recording cost is impossible without changing the public AiProvider contract. The OpenAiProvider::embed() (line 111-137) even discards the OpenAI usage object, extracting only data[0].embedding. The bug report itself concedes this is "partly structural."

2) Spec-conformant: PLANNING/10-fase-3-avancadas.md AI-006 (lines 411-424) shows the canonical AiManager calling record() only on the completion path; embed is merely "Similar: chat, embed, stream" with no cost-recording requirement. The cost_tracking feature's entire acceptance scope is built around AiCompletionResult.

3) Documented deviation: the AiManager class docblock explicitly states embed "deliberadamente pula cache... mas mantém o assert de limite" — embed deliberately keeps the assert.

4) Test codifies the behavior as correct: tests/Feature/AiManagerTest.php:104 'bypasses cache for embed() but still asserts the limit' asserts only the returned vector, with no record() expectation.

5) Not unbounded: every embed() call still runs assertWithinLimit(), so accumulated completion spend continues to gate embeddings. The only real gap is that embedding spend does not CONTRIBUTE to the tracked total (ledger undercounts) — an accounting-completeness limitation of negligible magnitude (embeddings cost ~$0.00002/1k tokens). Closing it is an enhancement requiring a public-contract change (extend embed() to return usage), i.e. deferred feature work, not a correction of broken behavior. Falls squarely under "reject documented/intended behavior + deferred features."
